meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' trap. Use this advanced SaaS starter kit checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to launch profitable apps faster.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' paralysis is a failure of market observation, not creativity. By leveraging a robust SaaS starter kit, you shift from ideation to execution. Use Antigravity to scaffold production-grade architecture and Claude Code to automate implementation, allowing you to validate micro-SaaS concepts in days, not months.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking like a countdown clock to irrelevance. The 'no project ideas' trap is the silent killer of potential empires; it’s not a lack of imagination, but a lack of framework. Most founders drown in the setup, burning their initial spark on boilerplate code rather than solving high-value user pain points.

But the game has changed. With high-velocity scaffolding and AI-driven implementation, you can bypass the 'blank canvas' phase entirely. We are moving from manual development to architectural orchestration. This checklist is your blueprint to stop searching for the perfect idea and start building the perfect engine to test your hypotheses in real-time.

steps.title: Define the Value-First Architecture
steps.description: Identify a recurring manual workflow in a niche B2B market. Use Antigravity to define the schema for a SaaS boilerplate that prioritizes billing and authentication first.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SaaS architecture schema for a B2B productivity tool. Include Stripe integration, Supabase auth, and a modular dashboard structure. Prioritize speed-to-revenue.

steps.title: Automate the Database Layer
steps.description: Don't write SQL manually. Use Claude Code to generate your Prisma or Drizzle schema based on the MVP feature set defined in step one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust relational database schema for a subscription-based SaaS. Include tables for users, teams, subscriptions, and core feature entities.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate prisma schema based on the business requirements in schema.md'

steps.title: Implement High-Conversion Auth
steps.description: Security is non-negotiable. Configure your authentication flow to minimize friction while ensuring enterprise-grade security for your future customers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure an authentication flow using NextAuth/Supabase with social login and email verification. Ensure it supports role-based access control (RBAC).
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement authentication middleware and protected routes'

steps.title: Integrate Payments as a Core Feature
steps.description: Your SaaS is a business, not a hobby. Connect Stripe webhooks to your application logic to handle subscription lifecycle events immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe webhook handler for subscription creation, upgrades, and cancellations. Map these to the database user status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'set up stripe webhook endpoints and sync with database'

steps.title: Deploy to Production-Ready Environment
steps.description: The final step is the first step of your business. Use Claude Code to configure your CI/CD pipeline for instantaneous deployment on push.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel/GitHub Actions pipeline that runs linting, testing, and deploys the production build.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure github actions for automated deployment'

pro_tips: Focus on 'boring' problems. The most profitable SaaS apps solve unsexy B2B workflow issues, not consumer novelties.
Ship the billing page before the dashboard. If they won't pay for it, you don't need to build it.
Use Claude Code to write your integration tests. Manual testing is a luxury you cannot afford as a solo founder.
Keep your tech stack minimal. The best SaaS is the one you can debug in 5 minutes at 2 AM.
Treat your codebase as an asset. Clean, modular code allows you to pivot your product direction without a complete rewrite.
content.next_steps: Once the boilerplate is deployed, spend 48 hours cold-emailing potential users in your niche. Your goal is not to sell, but to validate the problem. If they don't respond, pivot the feature set, not the architecture.
content.conversion_block: You now have the infrastructure to outpace 99% of your competitors. Stop waiting for inspiration and start building the foundation of your future revenue stream. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
